overview: Affinity is an aggressive U/R deck that leverages artifact creatures to rapidly reduce the mana cost of its spells. By flooding the board with cheap artifacts, you enable highly efficient removal and finishers while applying relentless pressure. The game plan revolves around maximizing artifact count to trigger affinity reduction and overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize.
win_conditions: Combat damage from a wide board of artifact creatures
Cheap, affinity-reduced finishers like Weapons Manufacturing
Token generation to accelerate artifact count and pressure

keep_criteria: Hands containing Mox Opal or other early ramp
Hands with Engineered Explosives or early removal
Hands with a clear path to turn 1 or 2 plays
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking artifact creatures or ramp
Hands with multiple removal spells but no threats

examples.reason: Provides turn 1 ramp, early creatures to reduce costs, and cheap removal.
Lacks artifact creatures and early ramp, making it impossible to trigger affinity reduction or apply early pressure.

early_game: Play Mox Opal on turn 1 for mana acceleration
Cast 1-2 mana artifact creatures to build affinity
Use Engineered Explosives to clear early blockers or face-up threats
mid_game: Flood the board with artifact creatures and tokens
Cast affinity-reduced threats and removal to maintain board control
Push damage consistently while protecting your board
late_game: Deploy high-impact finishers like Weapons Manufacturing at reduced cost
Use token generators to overwhelm opponent's removal
Close out the game with combat damage or direct damage spells

key_cards.name: Mox Opal
key_cards.notes: Provides early mana to cast artifact creatures and spells ahead of schedule. Requires an artifact creature in play to activate.

key_cards.name: Engineered Explosives
key_cards.notes: Highly efficient removal that becomes extremely cheap with affinity. Use to clear face-up threats or protect your board.

key_cards.name: Weapons Manufacturing
key_cards.notes: Generates a large number of artifact creature tokens, rapidly increasing board presence and affinity reduction for subsequent spells.

sequencing_tips: Play artifact creatures before activating Mox Opal to enable mana acceleration
Cast Engineered Explosives before playing your next creature to maximize value and protect threats
Hold removal until after you see the opponent's response to avoid wasting mana
Prioritize playing creatures that generate tokens or reduce costs further
Use affinity reduction to cast finishers earlier than expected, catching opponents off guard

matchups.advice: Apply maximum early pressure and play around counterspells. Flood the board with tokens to overwhelm removal.
Race them by pushing damage early. Use Engineered Explosives to clear their key threats before they stabilize.
Use cheap removal to clear their early threats and stabilize with artifact creatures. Race with token generation.
